commands.review_summary:
The branch changes `applyDoctorConfigMutation` to clear `pendingChanges` in repair mode and updates doctor repair sequencing tests to expect repaired mutations to have no pending changes.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests 0. Total 0 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: the patched false value flows into `finalizeDoctorConfigFlow`, which writes repaired config only when `shouldRepair && pendingChanges`. Existing config-flow coverage also expects a repair routed through this helper to keep `shouldWriteConfig: true`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor needs to repair the writeback regression and provide real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor-side proof gate for this external PR.

target.title: fix(doctor): only set pendingChanges=true when changes were previewed, not applied
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where `applyDoctorConfigMutation` in doctor config flow would always set `pendingChanges: true`, even when the mutation was already applied to `cfg` in repair mode (`shouldRepair: true`). The field's documented semantics state it should be "true when candidate differs from the persisted/effective config path", but after a repair, `cfg === candidate`, so the value contradicted its own definition.

While current downstream callers in `finalizeDoctorConfigFlow` guard on `shouldRepair` before checking `pendingChanges` (preventing visible misbehavior), the incorrect value creates confusion for future maintainers and risks misbehavior from new callers that check only `pendingChanges`.

## Why This Change Was Made

Changed `pendingChanges: true` to `pendingChanges: !params.shouldRepair` in `applyDoctorConfigMutation`, matching the existing `fixHints` logic which already correctly branches on `!params.shouldRepair`. Updated 8 test assertions across `config-mutation-state.test.ts` and `repair-sequencing.test.ts` to expect `false` instead of `true` after repair.

## User Impact

No user-visible impact. The `pendingChanges` flag is internal state used by doctor's config flow orchestration, and all existing downstream consumers already guard on `shouldRepair` before acting on `pendingChanges`. This fix aligns the code with its documented semantics.

## Evidence

Before: `config-mutation-state.ts:36` hardcoded `pendingChanges: true`.
After: `pendingChanges: !params.shouldRepair`, consistent with `fixHints` logic at line 37-40.

**Tests:**
```
pnpm test src/commands/doctor/
```
- 50 test files passed, 748 tests passed, 0 failed across 3 Vitest shards.

**Build:**
```
pnpm build
```
- Build completed successfully (exit code 0).
